uplink.description: Full 30-byte uplink: soil temp + GNSS fix + on-board ambient micro-climate (synthetic in-bounds; expected from upstream-codec.js field values, normalized)
uplink._note: Upstream decodeUplink returns {time_stamp:1742031012000, soil_temperature:22.5, soil_moisture:-45, soil_resistance:12345, ambient_temperature:19.8, ambient_humidity:56, ambient_pressure:1013, latitude:45.2105, longitude:-93.12345, battery:87}. soil_temperature->soil.temperature; soil_moisture is waterPotOffBoard (raw/-100, negative water potential, NOT a 0-100% moisture)->soilWaterPotential extra; ambient_*->air.temperature/pressure/relativeHumidity; latitude/longitude->position.*; battery (0-100 percent)->batteryPercent; time_stamp(ms)->time(RFC3339); soil_resistance/firmware->extras.

uplink.description: Short 21-byte uplink: soil temp + GNSS only, negative soil temperature, no on-board ambient block (synthetic in-bounds)
uplink._note: Upstream returns {time_stamp:1733040000000, soil_temperature:-3.25, soil_moisture:-120.5, soil_resistance:0, ambient_temperature:0, ambient_humidity:0, ambient_pressure:0, latitude:0, longitude:0, battery:100}. bytes[0..1]=0xFEBB is signed -325 (0.01 C) -> -3.25. The on-board ambient channels are zero-filled by upstream on a <26-byte payload; we omit air.* entirely (a zero air.pressure would violate the 900-1100 hPa vocabulary bound).
Upstream returns {errors:['unknown FPort']} for any fPort other than 152.

uplink.description: Truncated payload (fewer than 21 bytes) returns an error
uplink._note: Length guard; upstream would read undefined bytes and produce NaN lat/long and an invalid timestamp.
